---
title: "Architecture"
---

This page describes the layered architecture of `ts-agents` and how the components fit together.

## Layered model

The system is organized as a pipeline: CLI commands flow through a registry into sandboxed execution, producing durable artifacts that front-ends consume.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[CLI Contract<br/>ts-agents run/tool/data/skills/demo/agent] --> B[Tool Registry<br/>metadata, params, cost, limits]
  B --> C[Execution Layer<br/>local/subprocess/docker/daytona/modal]
  C --> D[Artifacts<br/>plots, JSON, reports, logs]
  D --> E[Front-Ends<br/>CLI agents, custom agents, Gradio]
```

## Tool execution flow

When you run a tool, this is the path from CLI to output:

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A["CLI: ts-agents run &lt;tool&gt; --run X --var Y"] --> B[Tool Registry Lookup]
  B --> C{Sandbox Mode?}
  C -->|local| D[In-process execution]
  C -->|subprocess| E[Separate Python process]
  C -->|docker/daytona/modal| F[Container / cloud sandbox]
  D --> G[Tool function runs]
  E --> G
  F --> G
  G --> H["Output: text + base64 PNG plots"]
  H --> I["--save → write text to file"]
  H --> J["--extract-images → decode PNGs to assets/"]
```

## Repository layout

```
ts-agents/
├── ts_agents/
│   ├── cli/              # Click CLI commands (run, tool, data, demo, skills, agent)
│   ├── tools/
│   │   ├── registry.py   # Central tool metadata: schemas, costs, categories
│   │   ├── agent_tools.py # _with_data wrappers that auto-load data + embed plots
│   │   └── *.py          # Individual tool implementations by category
│   ├── sandbox/          # Execution backends (local, subprocess, docker, daytona, modal)
│   ├── agents/           # Simple and deep agent implementations
│   └── ui/               # Gradio app components
├── skills/               # SKILL.md workflow definitions
│   ├── diagnostics/
│   ├── forecasting/
│   ├── decomposition/
│   ├── classification/
│   └── activity-recognition/
├── data/                 # Built-in test datasets
├── demo/                 # Demo runner scripts
├── docs/                 # Quarto documentation (this site)
│   └── talks/            # Presentation source (Quarto revealjs)
└── tests/                # Test suite
```

## Components

### CLI contract

One command surface for discovery (`tool`, `data`), execution (`run`), demos, skills, and agent entrypoints. The CLI is the stable API — everything else can change behind it.

::: {.callout-note}
## Design consequence
New tools are added once in the registry and immediately available through every front-end. No tool needs its own CLI subcommand.
:::

### Tool registry

Central metadata for tool schemas, costs, categories, and defaults. The registry enables:

- Consistent invocation across direct CLI usage and agent wrappers
- Cost-aware tool selection (agents can filter by `--max-cost`)
- Bundle-based tool sets (`minimal`, `standard`, `full`, `demo`)

### Execution layer

Backends provide portability and isolation:

| Mode | Isolation | Use case |
|------|-----------|----------|
| `local` | None (in-process) | Fastest; development and debugging |
| `subprocess` | Process-level | Safe default for untrusted inputs |
| `docker` | Container | Reproducible environments |
| `daytona` / `modal` | Cloud sandbox | Scale and remote execution |

::: {.callout-note}
## Design consequence
Switching sandbox mode is a single flag (`--sandbox docker`). Tool code does not change — only the execution wrapper differs.
:::

### Artifacts

Durable files are first-class outputs:

- **Analysis payloads** — JSON and text results
- **Visual assets** — base64-encoded plots, extractable to PNGs via `--extract-images`
- **Report artifacts** — Quarto documents (`.qmd`) rendered to PDF/HTML
- **Execution logs** — structured logs for debugging

::: {.callout-note}
## Design consequence
Operational debugging is file/log-driven, not chat-history-dependent. Benchmarks and workflows remain reproducible by replaying CLI commands.
:::

### Front-ends

Multiple front-ends share the same core:

- **Coding-agent runtimes** — Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Windsurf (skills exported per-agent)
- **Custom in-repo agents** — simple (single-turn) and deep (multi-step orchestrator)
- **Gradio app** — interactive UI for exploration

::: {.callout-note}
## Design consequence
Front-end changes never require algorithm rewrites. A new agent integration is just a skills export target.
:::
